steps.title: Enter the table in Desmos
steps.content: The table gives $(0,5)$, $(1,\frac{5}{2})$, $(2,\frac{5}{4})$, and $(3,\frac{5}{8})$. Enter the x-values and y-values as lists.

steps.title: Run an exponential regression
steps.content: Because each y-value is half the previous y-value, this is exponential decay. In Desmos, use the regression row $y_1\sim a b^{x_1}$. Desmos returns $a=5$ and $b=\frac{1}{2}$.

steps.title: Write and match the model
steps.content:
The model is:
$$f(x)=5\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^x$$
Since $\frac{1}{2}=2^{-1}$, this is also:
$$f(x)=5\cdot2^{-x}$$
That matches choice D, so D is correct.
